about.title: About patch-toolbox
about.subtitle: A public security knowledge repository — transparent, maintainable, and built for the community.
about.missionText: patch-toolbox is a long-term maintained security knowledge base collecting network vulnerabilities, system vulnerabilities, and common system troubleshooting information. All content is sourced from public channels and continuously organized.
about.valueOpenDataDesc: Sourced from public databases and community channels
about.valueDailyUpdatesDesc: Automated sync with validation and verification
about.valueDualFormatDesc: SQLite for programs, Markdown for humans
about.architectureDesc: Content is published in two parallel forms: structured SQLite databases for programmatic use, and readable Markdown documents for direct browsing.
about.sourceNvdDesc: The U.S. government repository of standards-based vulnerability management data.
about.sourceExploitDbDesc: Archive of public exploits and corresponding vulnerable software.
about.sourceGhsaDesc: Security vulnerability alerts for packages on GitHub.
about.sourceCisaDesc: Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog by the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency.
about.sourceStackDesc: Server Fault, Super User, AskUbuntu — community-verified troubleshooting knowledge.
about.sourceCommunityDesc: Community discussions and real-world troubleshooting experiences.
about.timeline2025Desc: Initial repository setup with network-security category.
about.timelineDailyDesc: Automated daily sync from all sources with CI validation.
about.timelineFutureDesc: Open for contributions and community curation.
about.ciBadgeDesc: Every commit is checked for structure, bilingual format, and database consistency.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.subtitle: Help us build the most comprehensive open security knowledge base. Every contribution matters.
contributing.reportIssuesDesc: Found outdated, incorrect, or missing vulnerability information? Open an issue on GitHub.
contributing.submitDataDesc: Contribute new vulnerability entries or troubleshooting solutions following our format guidelines.
contributing.improveCodeDesc: Help improve our sync scripts, validation tools, or the GitHub Pages site itself.
contributing.step1Desc: Create your own copy of patch-toolbox on GitHub.
contributing.step2Desc: Make a feature branch for your changes.
contributing.step3Desc: Follow the bilingual format. Technical details stay in original language.
contributing.step4Desc: Use the repository scripts to check your changes.
contributing.step5Desc: Open a pull request with a clear description of your changes.
contributing.rule1Desc: Headings and labels in Chinese / English pairs. Technical content stays in original language.
contributing.rule2Desc: Use CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, or N/A. Include CVSS score when available.
contributing.rule3Desc: Always cite the original source: NVD, Exploit-DB, GHSA, etc.
contributing.rule5Desc: Every entry must include mitigation or workaround information.
contributing.checkStructureDesc: Every PR is checked for correct directory structure, file naming, and required fields.
contributing.checkBilingualDesc: Ensures all entries have proper Chinese/English structural labels.
contributing.checkDbDesc: Verifies SQLite and Markdown representations stay in sync.
contributing.ctaDesc: Start by reading the full contributing guide on GitHub, or jump right in with a small fix.
